{"id":"project-LICHEN-6wx1","title":"Context Clone exposes cloned key material to multiple owners","description":"File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs
Line: 165

Context derives Clone:
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Zeroize)]
#[zeroize(drop)]
pub struct Context { ... }
```

The doc comment at lines 163-164 acknowledges this:
"Clone is intentionally supported for cases where multiple tasks need the context; both the original and clones will be zeroized when dropped."

However, cloning cryptographic contexts has subtle issues:
1. Both clones share the same sender_seq starting point, potentially causing nonce collision if both encrypt
2. Replay windows are also cloned, so replayed packets might be accepted by one clone but not another

Impact: Nonce reuse if cloned contexts are used concurrently for encryption.

Fix: Either remove Clone or document prominently that cloned contexts MUST NOT be used for concurrent encryption - only for concurrent decryption or for handing off to a single new owner.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:38:41.386389Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:38:41.386423Z"}

new_value: Fixed RNG no_std, CBOR tag skipping, Context clone docs, response replay note, Trickle saturating_add. Cargo tests pass, clippy clean. 3x subagent codereviews per bead completed in parallel.
